revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:淵神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Fuchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Fuchi Shrine.jpg | location = {{coord2|32|45|29.7|N|129|51|34.4|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Fuchi Shrine}} | Address = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, [[:en:Nagasaki City|Nagasaki City]], Fuchi-machi 8-1 | Deities = {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = | Festivals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Fuchi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|32|45|29.7|N|129|51|34.4|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|45|29.7|N|129|51|34.4|E}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Nagasaki|qid=Q38234|1=Nagasaki}} | coordinates= {{Coord|32.75825|N|129.859556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity= {{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|qid=Q10948069|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} }} '''Fuchi Shrine''' (淵神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Fuchi-machi, [[:en:Nagasaki City|Nagasaki City]], {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. Its former status under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is located on the western side of Nagasaki City, at the foot of {{ill|Mount Inasa|en|Mount Inasa|qid=Q3031841|lt=Mount Inasa|1=Mount Inasa}}. Adjacent to the shrine is the {{ill|Nagasaki Ropeway|en|Nagasaki Ropeway|qid=Q6958623|lt=Nagasaki Ropeway|1=Nagasaki Ropeway}}'s Fuchi Shrine Station, offering cable car access to the mountain summit. == Enshrined Deities == The primary enshrined deities are the {{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Goddesses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|lt=Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} Additionally, the shrine houses: * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi-no-Kami|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi-no-Kami|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113498050}} (Sugawara Ōkami) * {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Konpira Ōkami) == History == The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} enshrined as the tutelary deity of the Buddhist temple {{ill|Enmeiji|ja|延命寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmeiji|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486487}}, founded by Ryūsen, the temple's founder. In the [[Tenmon era]] (1532–1555), the shrine and temple were destroyed by Christians and remained unreconstructed until {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} dd (1634), when both were rebuilt. The Benzaiten shrine became the village guardian of Fuchi. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}, the {{ill|q|lt=Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}} led to the transformation of Benzaiten into the {{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Goddesses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} based on the principle of honji suijaku. The shrine was renamed "Fuchi Shrine." In May {{ill|1874|en|1874|qid=Q7761|lt=1874|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a village shrine, and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|qid=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. On [[August 9, 1945]], the shrine's main hall was destroyed during the {{ill|atomic bombing of Nagasaki|en|atomic bombing of Nagasaki|qid=Q4382340|lt=atomic bombing of Nagasaki|1=Atomic bombing of Nagasaki}}, and it was reconstructed in {{ill|1960|en|1960|qid=Q3754|lt=1960|1=1960}} (Shōwa 35). == Festivals == * October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Inasa Kunchi|ja|稲佐くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Kunchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == Grounds == * '''Hōju Inari Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|en|Toyokawa Inari|qid=Q3080596|lt=Toyokawa Inari|1=Toyokawa Inari}}, it is revered as a deity of prosperity and the arts. * '''Kuwa-hime Shrine''': Enshrines Kuwa-hime, a Christian woman believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1185032}}. Her memorial festival is held on August 7. * '''Takagi Inari Shrine''': Originally a household shrine of the [[Nagasaki magistrate]] Takagi family, later moved to the Mitsubishi Heavy Industries Nagasaki Shipyard Director's residence. It was relocated to the grounds of Fuchi Shrine in {{ill|1981|en|1981|qid=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56). * '''Jūnishi Shrine''': Dedicated to the twelve zodiac gods. * '''Hōju Kindergarten''': Located within the shrine grounds. == Other Features == File:Nagasaki Hiroshige Inasa.jpg|From the ''{{ill|Famous Views of the Sixty-odd Provinces|en|Famous Views of the Sixty-odd Provinces|qid=Q6724086|lt=Famous Views of the Sixty-odd Provinces|1=Famous Views of the Sixty-odd Provinces}}'': "Hizen Province, Nagasaki, Mount Inasa," by {{ill|Hiroshige|en|Hiroshige|qid=Q200798|lt=Utagawa Hiroshige|1=Hiroshige}} File:Fuchi Shrine Tourou.jpg|Lantern at Fuchi Shrine as depicted in ''Famous Views of the Sixty-odd Provinces'' and ''{{ill|Hokusai Manga|en|Hokusai Manga|qid=Q2489747|lt=Hokusai Manga|1=Hokusai Manga}}'' File:Ferrari Nagasaki.jpg|Ferrari ema (votive plaque) designed by {{ill|Ken Okuyama|en|Ken Okuyama|qid=Q3468094|lt=Ken Okuyama|1=Ken Okuyama}} and a Nagasaki Ropeway gondola he designed, dedicated to the shrine == Access == === Bus === * 1-minute walk from the "Ropeway-mae" bus stop, served by [[Nagasaki Bus]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[File:淵神社 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|Main Hall (photographed January 2005)]] {{translated page|ja|淵神社|version=102235266|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:淵神社]] {{Commons category|Fuchi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11560360}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nagasaki during the Edo period]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
